description: Guide to income tax refund processing: how refunds are issued, timelines, interest on delayed refunds (Section 244A at 6% p.a.), and steps to track or resolve refund issues.
timeline: Typically 15-90 days after ITR processing for e-filed and e-verified returns. Delayed refunds earn interest u/s 244A at 6% per annum.
howRefundArises: Refund = TDS (Form 16/16A credits) + TCS credit + Advance tax paid − Total tax liability. Shown automatically in Part B-TTI of ITR.

refundInterest.rate: 6% per annum (0.5% per month, simple interest)
refundInterest.startDateTimely: April 1 of Assessment Year (for returns filed by original due date)
refundInterest.endDate: Date of refund order passed by CPC/AO
refundInterest.noInterest: If refund is less than 10% of total tax assessed u/s 143(1)
refundInterest.taxability: Interest u/s 244A is itself taxable as income from other sources in the year of receipt

steps.description: Refund can only be credited to a pre-validated bank account linked to PAN. Go to e-filing portal → Profile → My Bank Account → Add/validate account. Takes 2-3 days for validation.
Check Schedule TDS1 (salary TDS), Schedule TDS2 (non-salary TDS), Schedule TCS (tax collected at source). All entries must match Form 26AS/AIS. Missing TDS entry = refund reduced.
After filing, refund amount shows in Part B-TTI. E-verify within 30 days. Refund processing begins only after e-verification.

Track on NSDL portal (tin.tin.nsdl.com/oltas/refundstatuslogin.html) or Income Tax portal under e-File → Income Tax Returns → Refund / Demand Status. Status progression: Return Submitted → Verified → Processing → Refund Issued.
Refund credited to pre-validated bank account via NEFT/RTGS. SMS + email notification sent. Usually takes 1-5 business days after processing.

commonIssues.resolution: Update bank account details on portal → Submit fresh pre-validation → Submit refund re-issue request under e-File → Refund Reissue

commonIssues.issue: Refund set off against old demand
commonIssues.resolution: Department can set off refund against any outstanding tax demand u/s 245. Portal sends intimation. If demand is incorrect, file rectification u/s 154.
Submit grievance on income tax portal under 'Grievance' section or contact Aaykar Sampark Kendra: 1800-103-0025 (toll free)

commonIssues.issue: TDS mismatch — refund lower than expected
commonIssues.resolution: Check Form 26AS vs ITR entries. If employer reported wrong TDS, contact employer for correction (TDS return revision). If portal issue, file rectification u/s 154.

refundSetOff.description: If outstanding tax demand exists against the taxpayer, the refund due may be partially or fully adjusted against that demand. CPC sends an intimation before set-off. Taxpayer can object within 30 days if demand is disputed.

commonMistakes: Not pre-validating bank account — refund cannot be issued
Missing TDS entries in ITR — reduces refund below entitlement
Wrong IFSC or account number — refund bounces back
Not tracking refund status — delayed issues go unnoticed
Ignoring Section 245 intimation — old demands may be set off without review
